item.title: India's Wipro unveils record buyback after slight quarterly revenue miss
item.description: India's Wipro announced a record share buyback of up to 150 billion rupees ($1.61 billion) on Thursday after ​reporting a slight quarterly revenue miss, as clients held back tech ‌spending in energy and banking sectors while business from key customer Estee Lauder slowed.

item.title: Travelers profit rises on stronger underwriting, lower catastrophe losses
item.description: Property and casualty insurance giant Travelers reported a jump in first-quarter profit, buoyed by robust underwriting gains and lower catastrophe ​losses than a year earlier when results were severely ‌hit by the Los Angeles wildfires.

item.title: Morning Bid: Six-week roundtrip
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Strong ASML, TSMC forecasts signal AI spending boom is intact
item.description: Strong forecasts from ASML and TSMC this week point to another quarter of hefty spending by American cloud-computing giants as they race to secure advanced chips ​needed for their artificial intelligence build-outs.

item.title: BNY's profit rises on strong fees, interest income growth​
item.description: BNY reported a rise in first-quarter profit on Thursday, as the world's largest custodian bank was helped by strong fee income ​and higher values of client assets.

item.title: Google should allow third-party search engines access to data, EU says
item.description: The European Commission has sent preliminary findings ​to Google on proposed ‌measures to comply with the EU's Digital Markets Act, which ​would allow third-party search ​engines to access Google search ⁠data, including that of ​artificial intelligence chatbots with search ​functionalities, the commission said on Thursday.

item.title: Trump tries economic reset as Republicans fret over high gas prices
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump will look to brush off concerns about the economy and the sagging political prospects of his Republican Party during a campaign-style swing through the battleground ​states of Nevada and Arizona this week, as the war with Iran pushes gasoline prices higher.

item.title: Fed leadership transition on shaky ground as clock ticks on Powell's term
item.description: The prospect of a smooth-and-on-time ​transition to U.S. President Donald Trump's pick for the next Federal Reserve chief, Kevin Warsh, looks increasingly to be on shaky ground, setting up a possible clash over who runs things in ‌the meantime.

item.title: US ESG shareholder resolutions plunge in face of Republican pressure, report says
item.description: Shareholders have filed 184 proposals promoting environmental, social and governance themes at U.S. companies so far this proxy season, a ​new study found, about half as many as last year, ‌as Republicans in Washington work to shift corporate power from investors to managers.

item.title: US will punish fraud, insider trading, derivatives regulator tells Congress
item.description: Washington's top derivatives regulator is due on Thursday to reassure lawmakers that the United States will punish fraud as concern mounts on Capitol Hill that oil, stock and ​prediction market players are illicitly trading on inside information from the White House, ‌according to prepared remarks seen by Reuters.

item.title: Wall Street bank earnings in five charts as volatile markets take center stage
item.description: Major U.S. banks delivered a split picture this week, as Middle East-linked market volatility lifted trading in the first quarter but clouded dealmaking prospects.

item.title: Hedge funds on track for best monthly returns in over a decade, Goldman says
item.description: Hedge ‌funds are on track for their best monthly performance in more than a decade, after overcoming a market downturn in March triggered by the Iran war that dragged returns lower, according ​to Goldman Sachs' latest quarterly hedge fund industry report.

item.title: Exclusive: Starlink outage hit drone tests, exposing Pentagon’s growing reliance on SpaceX
item.description: Last August, U.S. Navy officials carrying out a test of unmanned vessels realized they had hit a single point of failure: Starlink. A global outage across Elon Musk’s satellite network ​affecting millions of Starlink users had left two dozen unmanned surface vessels bobbing off the California coast, disrupting communications and halting operations for almost an hour.

item.title: PepsiCo tops quarterly revenue estimates as price cuts drive demand
item.description: PepsiCo beat analysts' estimates for quarterly revenue and stuck to its annual targets on Thursday, as recent price cuts for key snack ​brands helped revive demand in the U.S. and continued strength in its energy ‌drinks and prebiotic sodas.

item.title: Relaxation of US day-trading rules opens door to YOLO trading, higher risk
item.description: A regulatory move allowing smaller, everyday investors to engage in more day trading could spur impulsive, high-risk "YOLO", or "you-only-live-once," trades ​and allow eager individual traders to take an even bigger role in driving markets.

item.title: Boeing hiring more than 100 factory workers a week to grow output, replace retirees
item.description: Boeing ​is hiring around 100 to 140 factory workers a week, the highest pace since 2024, as the U.S. ‌jetmaker replaces retirees and increases staffing to support higher production rates and new models, a union leader said.

item.title: German banks examine risks of Anthropic's Mythos with authorities
item.description: German banks and national authorities are examining risks around Anthropic's new artificial intelligence model, an official said on ​Thursday, amid concerns that it could fuel cyberattacks.

item.title: Wall St futures edge higher on Mideast diplomacy hopes, strong earnings
item.description: U.S. stock index futures inched higher on Thursday ​as investors grew more confident that the worst of the Middle East conflict had passed, while a wave ‌of solid earnings allayed concerns about the economy.

item.title: Brazilian payments firm Ebanx makes Southeast Asia push
item.description: Brazilian cross-border payments company Ebanx said it is expanding global operations, with a focus on Southeast Asia, strengthening its footprint outside Latin ​America.

item.title: Pernod CFO says talks with Brown-Forman are "ongoing"
item.description: Pernod Ricard's talks to ​merge with U.S. ‌rival Brown-Forman are continuing, Pernod Ricard's ​finance chief said ​on Thursday.

item.title: EQT restarts sale of contact lens maker Ginko in at least $1 billion deal, sources say
item.description: Private equity firm EQT is restarting the sale process for the mainland Chinese business of ​contact lens maker Ginko International, targeting a valuation of ‌at least $1 billion, three people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Prince Harry praises Australia's leadership on social media ban as Meghan tells of online bullying
item.description: Britain's Prince Harry on Thursday praised Australia's "epic" leadership on curbing harmful social ‌media use for teens, as his wife Meghan, Duchess of Sussex, spoke of a decade of online abuse.

item.title: Zara owner Inditex reports unauthorised access to transaction databases
item.description: Zara owner Inditex said it had ​identified unauthorised access ‌to databases hosted by a third party containing ​information on transactions ​with customers.

item.title: Samsung asks court to block labour unions from holding a strike
item.description: Samsung Electronics asked a ​court to block ‌its South Korean labour unions from ​holding a strike, ​MoneyToday reported on Thursday.

item.title: Circle CEO sees 'tremendous opportunity' for yuan-backed stablecoin
item.description: Circle Internet Group sees "tremendous opportunity" for yuan-backed stablecoin as digital money becomes increasingly integrated into global trade and finance, said the chief of the world's largest regulated stablecoin issuer.

item.title: Hyundai Motor's US sales chief has left the company, Automotive News reports
item.description: Michael ​Orange, the ‌Hyundai Motor ​executive ​responsible for sales ⁠in ​all of ​the automaker's seven ​U.S. ​regions, left the ‌company ⁠on Tuesday, Automotive News ​reported.

item.title: Kering CEO pledges to double profit margins and revive Gucci brand​
item.description: Kering CEO Luca de Meo pledged to more than double the luxury group's operating profit ​margin and boost the appeal of its flagship brand Gucci as he sought to reassure investors unnerved by economic uncertainty and war in the ‌Middle East.

item.title: TSMC Q1 profit jumps 58% to record, beats expectations
item.description: TSMC, the world's largest contract ​chipmaker, posted a ‌58% jump in first-quarter net profit on Thursday, ​beating market forecasts ​and hitting a record, ⁠as it benefits ​from huge appetite globally ​for its artificial intelligence processors.

item.title: Pernod Ricard warns Iran war to hit full-year sales despite Q3 rebound
item.description: Pernod Ricard , which is in talks to merge with U.S. rival Brown-Forman , reported a stronger-than-expected 0.1% rise in sales for the third quarter but warned ​the decline in tourism due to the war in Iran would hurt its ‌travel retail business and impact full-year sales.

item.title: TomTom's reorganization boosts operating profit in Q1
item.description: Dutch digital mapping specialist TomTom ‌reported on Thursday higher than expected operating profit for the first-quarter of the year ​despite lower revenue following an ​internal reorganization.
